# **The Sticker Book**

All players will receive a sticker book item that, when right clicked, will open up a custom book GUI. Booths can register stickers via the [Booth Definition](?tab=t.5lmhbhoqu627), with the intention that stickers will be given to players for doing things in the booth, such as visiting it, finding a secret, or participating in some kind of activity. 

\[Stickers are rewarded via generated technical advancements, with the resource location summit.sticker\_book:\<namespace\>/\<sticker\_id\>. This is generated from the sticker fields in the Booth Definition. Grant this advancement to give a player the specified sticker. Do not overwrite this advancement.

Booths can register up to 5 stickers, and they’ll all be placed into the sticker book together, with the booth name above it. Unobtained stickers will automatically show a grey silhouette of the sticker. See below for an example of a page spread populated with the stickers of 5 different booths:

![][image1]  
*Note: The sticker book assets shown in the example are not complete. The layout will not change, but decorations are going to be added to the border of each page to represent the region of the booths on the page.*

The colour of the “tape” the stickers are placed on is dictated by the background\_color field in your booth definition. When stickers are hovered over, they will display the sticker’s obtained\_text if the sticker is obtained, otherwise it will display the sticker’s unobtained\_text.

![][image2]

When a player obtains a sticker, they will be shown a toast in the top right of their screen.

# Style Guide

Stickers are relatively flexible in terms of size and style, but we do have some guidelines:

* Stickers must use a square canvas (eg: 100x100).  
* Stickers should be at least 20x20. This is the resolution they will be scaled to in the sticker book, if you want to make your stickers pixel consistent, use this resolution. Stickers larger than 256x256 will be scaled down to 256x256.  
* Try to make your stickers be as large as possible on their canvas, otherwise they may appear somewhat small in the sticker book.  
* Alpha values will be preserved.  
* Try to make your stickers look like stickers, giving them a white border can go a long way.

[image1]: <data:image/png;base64,>

[image2]: <data:image/png;base64,>
